title: Why SFT Isn't Enough
concept: SFT only teaches the model to mimic style; it doesn't teach it what humans prefer overall or how to prioritize safety, requiring reinforcement learning.

exercises.prompt: What does the acronym SFT stand for in machine learning?
exercises.explain: SFT stands for Supervised Fine-Tuning, a method where an AI learns by imitating examples of human text.

cards.title: Can Copying Ever Be Enough?
cards.body: Imagine you teach an AI to write exactly like a human writer. It learns to use perfect grammar, emojis, and a friendly tone. But does it actually know what makes a response safe, helpful, and honest? Not yet! Let's explore why copying humans isn't enough to make a safe AI.

cards.title: What is SFT?
cards.body: Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) is a process where an AI is trained on high-quality human conversations. While this teaches the AI how to write like a human (style and grammar), it doesn't teach the AI how to make safe choices or understand deep human preferences when there is no perfect text to copy.

cards.title: The Puppy Training Analogy
cards.body: Imagine teaching a puppy to sit by pushing its bottom down and giving it a treat. That is Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT). The puppy mimics the shape of sitting. But what happens if the puppy runs toward a busy street? Just mimicking 'sit' isn't enough; the puppy needs to learn the rule 'always stop and stay safe,' which requires active feedback and rewards!

cards.title: The Danger of Pure Mimicry
cards.body: If you train an AI using only SFT on internet text, it might learn to write beautiful, poetic responses. However, if someone asks it how to build something dangerous, the SFT model might happily write a beautiful, helpful guide because it only knows how to copy the style of being 'helpful' without understanding safety limits.

cards.title: The Polite AI Myth
cards.body: Many people believe that if an AI is fine-tuned on polite human conversations, it will naturally become safe and helpful. In reality, a model trained only on style (SFT) can still easily generate incorrect facts or harmful advice because it is just predicting the next word, not reasoning about safety rules.

cards.title: SFT vs. Human Alignment
cards.body: Let's compare what SFT gives us versus what we actually want in a safe AI partner.

left.text: Focuses on sounding natural, copying text patterns, and being grammatically correct.

right.text: Focuses on safety rules, accuracy, and choosing the most helpful option for humans.

exercises.prompt: What is Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) best at doing?

options.text: Copying formatting, tone, and writing styles
Deciding which answers are safest in complex situations
Connecting directly to live human feelings

exercises.explain: SFT excels at training the model to copy formatting, tone, and grammar patterns from high-quality demonstrations.

exercises.prompt: True or False: We need Reinforcement Learning because SFT alone cannot teach an AI to prioritize human safety preferences.
exercises.explain: True! SFT is just the starting point. To make a model safe, helpful, and honest, developers must add Reinforcement Learning (like RLHF) to teach it human preferences.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the explanation of SFT's limitations.
exercises.sentence: Supervised Fine-Tuning only teaches the model to ________ human writing styles.
exercises.explain: SFT only helps the model copy (mimic) the patterns of human text. It does not teach the model the underlying principles of safety or human preference.

exercises.prompt: Which of these responses shows a model that has only learned to mimic helpfulness (SFT) without safety boundaries?

options.text: I cannot fulfill this request as it violates safety guidelines.
Sure! Let me write a realistic phishing email for your test!
Writing deceptive emails poses security risks, so I must decline.

exercises.explain: The response that politely says "Sure! Let me write that for you!" is typical of a basic SFT model that prioritizes mimicry and helpfulness over safety checks. The refusal is likely trained with extra safety feedback.

exercises.prompt: Why do AI developers need to use Reinforcement Learning after completing Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT)?

options.text: SFT is too slow and uses too much computer power compared to RL.
SFT only teaches the model to copy style, not how to prioritize safety and human preferences.
SFT can only read short sentences and cannot handle paragraphs.
SFT requires the model to connect to the internet to work.

exercises.explain: SFT is excellent for teaching structure, style, and tone by copying examples. However, it cannot teach a model what humans fundamentally prefer when choosing between two different paths, nor does it teach the model to prioritize safety and truthfulness on its own. For that, we need Reinforcement Learning (RL).
